danger_signals.visual_form: playing arena boundary drops downward, bubbles move closer to bottom line
danger_signals.meaning: increasing time pressure; game over condition approaches

danger_signals.visual_form: tempo increase in background music as bubbles near bottom
danger_signals.meaning: critical danger state; player failure imminent if bubbles cross bottom line

danger_signals.visual_form: any bubble reaches or passes the bottom boundary of playing arena
danger_signals.meaning: immediate game over; failure state reached

safety_signals.visual_form: three or more identically colored bubbles cluster together
safety_signals.meaning: player can eliminate bubbles; clearing arena progress

safety_signals.visual_form: matched bubbles and dependent bubbles vanish from screen; points awarded
safety_signals.meaning: successful action; arena space freed; progress toward level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: all bubbles removed from playing field
safety_signals.meaning: level complete; advance to next round with new bubble pattern

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: pointer device at bottom of screen

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: descending ceiling; randomly generated bubble colors; level-specific bubble layouts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: relaxed spacing; bubbles distributed across upper arena; slow/normal music tempo
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player has time to make strategic decisions; automatic firing does not activate immediately

world_state_to_visual.world_state: mid-round pressure (ceiling descending, bubbles approaching middle)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ceiling visibly lower; bubbles compressed vertically; music tempo increases noticeably
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must act more quickly; fewer safe positions remain; cascade effects from matches become more consequential

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: bubbles clustered near bottom boundary; music tempo fast/intense; visual proximity signals threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: automated bubble fire activates if player idle; single mistake causes failure; all actions high-consequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: arena emptied; all bubbles removed; transition to next level screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: new bubble pattern loads; ceiling resets to top; music returns to baseline tempo

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: bubble crosses bottom line; arena frozen; failure state display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player loses; input disabled; game reset or continue option presented

environmental_storytelling.example: rectangular arena with clear top/bottom boundaries; bottom line serves as visual threshold for failure; sidewalls indicate bounce surfaces for physics behavior
bubbles of identical color must cluster to be removed; color distribution visible at round start tells experienced players difficulty/strategy requirements
ceiling descent creates descending visual threat; player watches safe space diminish; music acceleration correlates with visual compression
match success removes bubbles (visual relief); ceiling drops regularly (visual threat); music tempo responds to state (audio-visual correlation)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: removal from field; dependent/hanging bubbles also removed; points awarded; ceiling does not drop during this action
descends one increment downward; all attached bubbles move down proportionally; descent speed/frequency influenced by remaining color count
automatic bubble fire triggers; selected bubble fires up arena in straight line
travels in straight line; bounces off sidewalls; stops when touching other bubbles or reaching arena top
tempo accelerates; intensity increases; signals critical time pressure without text

notes: Bust-A-Move uses pure mechanical/visual communication for world-state. No faction narrative, dialogue, or political systems exist. Danger/safety entirely conveyed through spatial positioning (bubble proximity to bottom line), temporal pressure (ceiling descent rate, music speed), and color matching mechanics. The game relies on abstract puzzle grammar: color identity = match target; bottom line = absolute failure boundary; ceiling descent = automated difficulty escalation. Environmental storytelling is minimal but functional: the arena itself is a closed system that communicates all necessary state information. Behavioral tells are deterministic/rule-based rather than character-driven. This is a pure puzzle paradigm with no world-state beyond arena configuration.
sources_used: tcrf - Bust-A-Move (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
